text.title: SLO Definition — Output Contract
text.summary: Schema for slo-defs.yaml and the per-SLO rationale doc.

forbidden.pattern: class field empty OR not in enum
sli_set with >6 entries (class template + 2 extras max for most classes)
budget_policy_path missing OR points to non-existent file

allowed-transformations.transformation: Add service-specific SLIs within the 2-extra cap; document why the class template was insufficient.
Tighten the SLO target after demonstrated headroom (≥3 consecutive windows hitting target with budget unspent).
Loosen the target only with product re-signoff; record the loosening event.

section.title: SLI / SLO / SLA distinction

list.item: SLI (Service Level Indicator): the metric. e.g. request_success_rate.
SLO (Service Level Objective): the internal target. e.g. 99.9% over 30d rolling.
SLA (Service Level Agreement): the external contractual obligation, looser than SLO, with penalties. e.g. 99.5% over the contract month.

section.p: Keep SLO ≥ SLA + safety margin. SLA breaches require legal/comms; SLO breaches require engineering/product attention.

list.item: Class assigned; SLI set fits class template + ≤2 extras
Each SLO has a user-acceptable rationale and a named owner
Budget policy doc exists at the linked path
